text:
Once upon a time, Tim went to school. He had a big smile on his face. “I am happy!” he said. His teacher, Miss Lily, saw him and said, “I am happy too, Tim! Today we will learn new things!”

In the classroom, Tim sat with his friends. “What will we learn today?” Tim asked. His friend Mia answered, “We will learn about colors!” Tim clapped his hands. “Yay! I love colors!” he said with joy.

Miss Lily showed them red, blue, and yellow. “Look! This is red,” she said, holding a red apple. “Can you say ‘red’?” The kids all shouted, “Red!” Tim felt silly and laughed. “I can say it!” he cheered.

After learning, it was time to play outside. “I am so happy!” Tim said, running with his friends. They played games and laughed together. At the end of the day, Tim told Miss Lily, “I learned so much today!” She smiled and said, “I am proud of you, Tim!” And Tim felt very happy.

Anna was a happy girl. She loved her classroom. It was bright and colorful. The walls were yellow, and the sun came in the big windows. Anna loved to draw with her crayons and make pretty pictures.

One day, Anna saw her friend Tom. He looked sad. He could not find his blue crayon. Anna wanted to help him. She said, "Tom, do you want to use my blue crayon?" Tom smiled and said, "Yes, please!" Anna gave him the crayon, and he was happy again.

With the blue crayon, Tom drew a big, blue car. Anna drew a bright sun and green grass. They worked together and made a nice picture. The teacher saw their work and smiled. She said, "Wow! You two are great friends!"

Anna was very happy. She helped Tom, and they had fun. The classroom was bright, and so were their smiles. Anna learned that helping others can make everyone feel good.

One sunny day at school, Sam was very happy. He liked to read books. In the morning, Sam said, “I want to read a nice book!” His teacher smiled and said, “That is kind, Sam. Reading is fun!”

After reading, Sam went to the playground with his friends. They saw swings and slides. Sam said, “Let’s play on the swings!” His friend Lily said, “Yes! Let’s swing high!” They pushed each other and laughed.

Then, Sam and Lily saw a boy sitting alone. Sam said, “Come play with us!” The boy smiled and said, “Okay!” They all played together on the swings and slides. Everyone was happy.

At the end of the day, Sam felt glad. He said, “I had fun today! I like to read and play with friends.” Lily nodded and said, “Me too!” They went home with smiles on their faces.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a girl named Lucy. Lucy liked to learn and play with her friends. Every day, her kind teacher would help the kids. The teacher was very nice and always made the class feel happy.

One day, the teacher asked the kids to write a letter. Lucy was excited! But when she saw her friend Tim, he looked sad. He did not know how to write the letter. Lucy wanted to help him. She smiled and said, "Don't worry, Tim! I can help!"

Lucy and Tim sat together. They worked in a quiet corner of the room. Lucy showed Tim how to write each word. Tim smiled big as he wrote. Soon, he finished his letter. He thanked Lucy and felt very happy.

When the teacher saw Tim's letter, she clapped her hands. "Great job!" she said. Tim grinned and felt proud. Lucy felt proud too. Helping her friend made her heart feel warm. They both smiled, and the whole class was happy.

Jake was a little boy. He had a shy smile and liked to play. One day at school, Jake saw his friend Lily. Lily was sad. She looked at her desk and said, “Oh no! I lost my pencil!”

Jake wanted to help. He said, “Don’t worry, Lily! I will help you find it.” Jake and Lily started to look. They looked under the table, in the backpack, and on the floor. Jake ran fast. He wanted to find the pencil for his friend.

After a few minutes, Jake saw something shiny. “Look, Lily! I see it!” he said with a happy voice. He ran to the pencil and picked it up. “Here it is!” he shouted. Lily smiled big and said, “Thank you, Jake! You are a good friend!”

Jake felt happy. He helped his friend, and now they could play together. The shy boy and his friend laughed and had fun all day at school. It was a good day!

Once upon a time, in a sunny school, there was a girl named Mia. Mia was a curious girl. She liked to ask questions and learn new things. One day, Mia saw a big book on a shelf. It looked very fun!

Mia wanted to play with the book. She took it down and opened it. Inside, there were pictures of animals, trees, and stars. Mia's eyes got big! She smiled and said, “Wow! This is great!” She began to point at the pictures and make animal sounds. 

Mia's friends saw her playing with the book. They came over to see what was so fun. Mia said, “Look at the big cat!” Her friends giggled and joined her. They all had fun together, looking at the pictures and making sounds. They played and shared the book. 

At the end of the day, all the kids felt happy. They learned that sharing is fun and makes playtime better. Mia was glad she shared the book. She smiled and said, “Let’s play again tomorrow!” And they all agreed.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ben. He was a brave boy who went to school every day. Ben loved his school and his friends. One day, Ben saw his friend, Sam, looking sad at his desk. 

Ben walked over and asked, "What is wrong, Sam?" Sam said, "I don't have a pencil to write." Ben wanted to help. He had many pencils in his bag. "You can use my pencil," Ben said with a big smile. He was happy to share. 

Sam's face lit up. He took the pencil and said, "Thank you, Ben! You are very kind." Ben felt warm inside. He loved helping his friend. They both wrote together at the desk and laughed. 

At the end of the day, Ben and Sam played together with all their friends. Ben was brave and made Sam happy. They learned that sharing is nice, and it makes friends feel good. Everyone left school with a big smile!

Zoe went to school with a big smile. It was a sunny day, and she was ready to learn and play. At lunchtime, Zoe saw her friend Mia. Mia looked sad. Zoe walked over and asked, "What is wrong, Mia?"

"I lost my small lunch," Mia said, with tears in her eyes. Zoe felt bad for her friend. Zoe wanted to help! "Don't worry, Mia. I can share my lunch with you!" Zoe said. She opened her lunchbox and took out a yummy sandwich and an apple. 

Mia's face lit up with joy. "Thank you, Zoe! You are so nice!" They sat together and ate lunch. They giggled and talked about fun games. Zoe felt happy to help her friend. 

When lunch was done, Zoe and Mia played outside. They ran and laughed under the big, blue sky. Zoe felt warm inside because she helped someone. It was a great day at school, and they were both happy!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Leo. Leo had a red backpack. He loved to go to school every day. At school, Leo liked to play and learn with his friends. 

One sunny day, Leo saw a girl named Mia. She looked sad. Leo walked over and asked, "What is wrong, Mia?" Mia said, "I can't reach the top shelf. My book is up high!" Leo wanted to help. He was gentle and kind. 

Leo picked up a chair and stood on it. He reached up and got Mia's book. "Here you go!" he said with a big smile. Mia was so happy! "Thank you, Leo!" she said. 

After that, Leo and Mia sang a song together. They sang about helping friends and being kind. The other kids joined in, and soon everyone was singing. The school was full of happy smiles and laughter. Leo felt great because he helped his friend. It was a wonderful day!

Ella was a clever girl. She loved to play at school with her friends. One sunny day, the teacher said, “Let’s build a big tower!” Ella was very excited. She wanted to help.

Ella took a big box of blocks. Her friends took some too. They were all ready to build. But then, they saw that they did not have enough blocks. Ella felt sad. "How can we build a big tower now?" she asked.

Then, Ella had a nice idea. “Let’s use our crayons!” she said. "We can draw a tower!" Her friends smiled. They took out their crayons and began to draw. Ella drew the base. Her friend Sam drew the middle. They worked together and made a tall, colorful tower on paper.

When they showed their drawing to the teacher, she smiled big. “What a clever idea!” she said. Ella and her friends felt happy. They learned that when you work together and use your minds, you can make something great! The class had fun all day and played with their crayons, happy and proud of their teamwork.

Once upon a time, in a cheerful school, there was a boy named Max. Max loved to paint. One sunny day, his teacher said, “Let’s make a big picture for the library!” All the kids cheered. They were excited to paint together!

Max said, “I want to use bright colors!” His friend Lily smiled and said, “Me too! Let’s make it beautiful!” They picked red, blue, and yellow paint. They laughed and helped each other. Max painted a big sun, and Lily painted flowers. They worked hard and had so much fun!

When they finished, the picture was very pretty! The teacher clapped and said, “Great job, everyone! Now, let’s put it in the library!” Max felt happy. He liked his school and his friends. They all looked at their picture and smiled.

The moral of the story is that working together makes fun! Max learned that when friends help each other, they can make something great. And they all lived happily in their cheerful school.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. Lily was a friendly girl who loved to play with her friends at school. One sunny day, they had homework to do. The teacher asked everyone to count flowers in the garden for their homework. 

Lily wanted to help her friend Max. Max was sad because he did not know how to count. Lily smiled and said, "Don’t worry, Max! I can help you!" They both went to the garden. There were many pretty flowers. 

Lily and Max saw red flowers, yellow flowers, and blue flowers. Together, they counted each flower. "One, two, three, four..." they said, giggling as they counted. Max felt happy because he learned how to count with Lily’s help. 

After they finished counting, Lily and Max ran back to class. They were excited to tell their teacher about all the flowers they counted. The teacher was very proud. Lily felt good for helping a friend. They both smiled, knowing they did their homework together. It was a great day at school!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tom. Tom loved to draw. He liked to make pictures of trees, cars, and tall buildings. One sunny day at school, Tom wanted to show his drawing to his friends.

After class, Tom saw his friends playing outside. They were having fun and skipping around. Tom felt a little shy, but then he thought, “I want to show them my drawing!” So, he took a deep breath and skipped over to his friends.

“Look at my drawing!” Tom said with a big smile. His friends stopped and looked. “Wow! That is a tall building!” they said. Tom felt happy. They all wanted to see more of his drawings. They sat together, and Tom showed them all his pictures. 

Tom learned that it was good to share. When he shared his drawing, he made his friends happy, and they had fun together. They all played and drew, and they laughed a lot. At the end of the day, Tom felt very joyful because sharing made everyone smile.

One sunny day at school, Emma sat in a big circle with her friends. The teacher smiled and said, "Today, we will listen to a happy song!" The sun shone bright, and the room felt warm. Emma felt excited.

The teacher played the song, and it was fun. Emma smiled and sang along. "I love this song!" she said. Her friend Mia said, "Me too! It makes me so happy!" They all clapped their hands and danced. 

Then, Emma saw her friend Sam. He looked sad. "What’s wrong, Sam?" Emma asked. Sam said, "I can’t hear the song well." Emma wanted to help. "Come sit by me!" she said. Sam smiled and sat next to Emma. 

Now, Sam could hear the song better. "Thank you, Emma!" he said. Emma grinned. They all sang together. The song was loud, and their hearts were warm. At the end, the teacher said, "Great job, everyone!" Emma felt happy because she helped her friend.

One sunny day at school, Jack and his friend Mia sat at a big table. They had a big puzzle to put together. Jack looked at the pieces and said, "Look, Mia! This one is blue!" 

Mia giggled. "Yes, it is! But where does it go?" Jack scratched his head. "Hmm, let’s try!" They both tried to fit the blue piece, but it did not fit. 

Jack made a silly face and said, "I am a puzzle master!" Mia laughed and said, "You are so funny, Jack!" They both laughed and tried again. 

With happy smiles, they worked together. After a while, they put the last piece in the puzzle. "We did it!" shouted Jack. Mia clapped her hands. "Yay! We are the best puzzle team!" They high-fived each other and felt proud. In the end, they were happy friends with a big, bright puzzle.

Sofia was a little girl. She loved to explore her school. One day, her teacher said, "We will make a big painting!" Sofia was very excited. She wanted to help her friends.

Sofia saw her friend Max looking sad. "What is wrong, Max?" she asked. "I don’t know how to paint," he said with a frown. Sofia smiled and said, "I can help you!" They sat together with bright colors and big brushes.

Sofia showed Max how to make fun shapes. They laughed and painted happy faces and tall trees. Soon, all their friends joined them. They worked together and made a big, bright painting. Sofia felt proud of their work.

At the end of the day, the teacher hung the painting on the wall. "Look at what we made!" she said. Sofia and Max smiled big smiles. They felt happy to help each other and be friends. The school was full of joy, and everyone loved the painting!

One sunny day at school, a boy named Will was very excited. He was playing with his friends in the big room. They liked to play many fun games. Will had an idea. "Let’s find a new game!" he said with a big smile.

Will and his friends looked all around the room. They searched in the boxes and under the tables. They found toys and books, but no new game. Will did not give up. He kept looking. Then, he saw a shiny box on a high shelf. "What is that?" he asked.

Will reached up and opened the box. Inside, he found a game with bright cards and a big board. "Wow! Look!" Will shouted. His friends ran over, and they all cheered. They had discovered a new game! Everyone was very excited to play.

They sat on the floor and started the game. They took turns and laughed a lot. Will felt happy when his friends smiled and played with him. In the end, they all agreed it was the best day ever. They had fun, and they were glad they discovered the new game together.

Nora was a little girl who went to school. One day, her teacher said they would have a lesson on animals. Nora loved animals, but she felt a little scared because she did not know much about them. 

The lesson started, and the teacher showed pictures of big cats, small dogs, and funny birds. Nora listened carefully. She saw a picture of a big, furry bear. It looked so big and strong! Nora thought bears were scary. She was still scared, even when the lesson was almost done.

When the lesson finished, the teacher asked if anyone had questions. Nora raised her hand and said, “Bears are big and scary! Are they nice?” The teacher smiled and said, “Yes, bears are nice when they are not close to people. They want to be safe too.”

Nora felt happy when she learned that bears were not so scary after all. She found out that all animals have their own homes and families. Now, Nora was not scared anymore. She loved the lesson about animals and wanted to learn more!

Dan was a little boy who went to school. One day, he felt very lonely. He sat on a bench and looked around. Other kids were playing, but Dan did not play. He wished he had someone to talk to.

Then, Dan saw a girl named Mia. She was sad and sitting by herself too. Dan remembered a story his mom told him about helping others. He thought it would be nice to help Mia feel better. So, he walked over to her with a big smile.

"Hi, Mia! Do you want to play with me?" Dan asked. Mia looked up and smiled a little. "Yes, please!" she said. They played games together and shared toys. Soon, they were both laughing and happy.

Now, Dan did not feel lonely anymore. He helped Mia, and she helped him. They became great friends. When school was over, they waved goodbye, knowing they would play again tomorrow.

One day at school, Ivy felt nervous. She had a big paper to show to her class. Ivy loved to help her friends, but now she was not sure if she could do it. 

Ivy looked at her friend Max. He was smiling and waving. "You can do it, Ivy!" he said. Ivy took a deep breath. She felt a little better. Max was her good friend, and he always helped her feel happy. 

When it was Ivy's turn, she stood up. Her hands were shaking, but she smiled. She held up the paper and said, "This is my favorite picture!" The kids clapped and cheered. Ivy felt strong and brave. 

After class, Max gave Ivy a big hug. "You did great!" he said. Ivy smiled wide. She was no longer nervous. She was happy because she helped her class, and she had a good friend.

One sunny day, Kai went to school. He saw his friend, Mia. “Hi, Mia!” Kai said with a big smile. “Are you happy today?”

“Yes, I am! I want to learn!” Mia replied. They ran to their classroom. The teacher, Mr. Lee, was there. “Good morning, class!” he said. “Today, we will learn about animals!” 

Kai raised his hand. “I love animals!” he said. Mr. Lee smiled. “What animal do you like, Kai?” 

“I like dogs!” shouted Kai. “Dogs are fun!” Mia said, “I like cats!” 

They learned about dogs and cats. They drew pictures and sang songs. At the end of the day, Kai said, “I am so happy! I learned a lot!” Mia nodded and said, “Me too!” They left school with big smiles on their faces.

Ruby was a happy girl. She loved to go to school. In her bright classroom, there were many fun things to do. She liked to play, listen, and learn with her friends. But best of all, Ruby loved to draw. 

One day, Ruby took out her big paper and her colorful crayons. She wanted to draw a big tree with lots of green leaves. Ruby started to draw, but then she saw her friend, Max. Max looked sad because he could not draw well. Ruby had an idea! 

"Max, come and sit with me!" she said. "I can help you!" Max smiled. Ruby showed him how to hold the crayon and how to draw a tree. Soon, Max was drawing too, and he was happy. The classroom was bright with the colors of their pictures.

When they finished, their teacher saw their work. "What nice trees!" she said. Ruby and Max were proud. Ruby learned that helping friends is good. They both smiled, and the classroom felt even brighter.

Finn was a happy boy. He loved to go to school. One day, Finn said, "I want to read a book!" His teacher smiled and said, "That is kind, Finn. Let’s find a good book."

After reading, it was time to go to the playground. Finn ran fast to the swings. "Come and play!" he called to his friends. They all joined him and had so much fun.

One of Finn’s friends fell down and cried. Finn ran to him and said, "It is okay! You are safe now." He gave him a hug. Finn's friend smiled and said, "Thank you, Finn. You are very kind."

After playing, the bell rang. Finn and his friends went back to class. Finn felt happy because he had read a nice book and helped his friend. The moral of the story is: being kind makes everyone happy!

Luna was a little girl. She liked school very much. Her teacher was kind and smiled a lot. One day, it was very quiet in the room. The sun shined through the window. Luna looked at her desk and saw a nice, big paper.

Luna wanted to write on the paper. She took out her blue crayon and started to make big, happy lines. She wrote the sun, the sky, and her cat. She was so proud! The teacher walked by and saw what Luna wrote. “What a great job, Luna!” the teacher said.

Luna felt so happy. She wanted to show her friends. She turned around and said, “Look! Look at my paper!” Her friends clapped their hands and smiled. They all wanted to write, too. 

The teacher said, “Let’s all write together!” The room was still quiet, but it was happy now. Luna and her friends wrote their own stories. When the bell rang, they all laughed and shared their work. Luna had a fun day at school.

Owen was a shy boy. He liked to sit alone with his toys at school. One day, he found a blue pencil on the floor. It was shiny and bright. Owen picked it up and looked around. He wanted to find out who it belonged to.

Owen saw a girl named Mia. She was sitting by herself, too. She looked shy, just like him. Owen took a deep breath and said, "Hi! Is this your pencil?" Mia looked at him and smiled. "Yes! Thank you!" she said. Owen felt happy to help her.

Mia took the pencil, but then she asked, "Do you want to play with me?" Owen's heart raced. He was nervous, but he really wanted to have a friend. He said, “Yes!” and they started to run around the playground together. They laughed and played games. 

From that day, Owen was not shy anymore. He had a new friend, and they played every day. They found more toys and pencils to share. Owen felt warm and happy inside. School was fun now!

Maya was a happy girl. She loved to go to school and see her friends. One sunny day, in class, Maya saw a big book on the shelf. The book was very pretty, and Maya was curious about what was inside.

Maya called her friend, Sam. “Come look at this book!” she said. Sam ran over with a big smile. They both opened the book and saw bright pictures of animals. “Let’s play a game!” said Sam. Maya nodded and they began to play. They made funny sounds like the animals in the book.

Maya and Sam took turns pointing at the pictures. They laughed and giggled. Other kids joined them too. Everyone wanted to play! The classroom was full of joy and fun. Maya felt happy to share the book with her friends. 

At the end of the day, Maya and Sam hugged. “This was the best day!” said Maya. “Yes, it was!” said Sam. They promised to play together again tomorrow. They walked home, feeling warm and happy inside.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Cole. Cole was a brave boy. He liked to play with his friends at school. One day, Cole saw a new boy sitting alone at a small desk. The new boy looked sad. Cole wanted to help him.

Cole walked over and said, “Hi! My name is Cole. Do you want to play with me?” The new boy smiled and said, “Yes! My name is Sam.” Cole felt happy. He wanted to share his toys with Sam. Together, they played with blocks and built a big tower.

When they finished, Cole said, “Let’s share the tower with the other kids!” Sam thought that was a good idea. They called their friends over and showed the tower. Everyone clapped and cheered. Cole and Sam felt like heroes.

At the end of the day, Cole and Sam were best friends. They learned that sharing is fun and makes everyone happy. Cole was brave to talk to Sam, and now he had a friend to play with at school.

Chloe went to school one sunny day. She had a small lunchbox with yummy food inside. Chloe loved her lunch, but she saw her friend Mia looking sad. Mia had no lunch. 

Chloe said, "Do you want some of my lunch?" Mia smiled and nodded. Chloe was happy to help. She took out her small sandwich and her fruit. She shared her food with Mia. 

Mia ate the food and felt much better. "Thank you, Chloe!" said Mia. They both laughed and played together. Chloe felt good inside because she helped her friend. 

At lunch time, they sat together. They talked and shared their small stories. Chloe and Mia had a great time and were very happy. They learned that helping others is fun!

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Jude. Jude had a gentle smile and a big, red backpack. Every day, he took his backpack to school, and he loved to learn and play with his friends.

One sunny day, Jude saw a girl named Mia. She looked sad because she lost her toy. Jude wanted to help her, so he said, “Don’t worry, Mia! We can look for it together.” They both searched the playground, looking under the swings and behind the slide.

While they looked, Jude began to sing a happy song. The song was about finding lost things. Soon, other kids joined in and sang too! They all sang together and laughed, which made Mia smile. Even though they did not find the toy, everyone felt happy.

At the end of the day, Mia thanked Jude for being a good friend. Jude smiled back and said, “It’s nice to help!” They hugged, and Jude felt warm inside. As he walked home with his red backpack, he sang a gentle song about friendship.

Leah was a clever girl. She loved to draw and play with her friends at school. One day, Leah wanted to build a big tower with her crayons. She took out her red crayon, her blue crayon, and her yellow crayon. She was very excited!

Leah started to build. She stacked her crayons one on top of the other. But oh no! The tower fell down! Leah felt sad. She wanted to make a tall tower, but it kept falling. Her friend Max saw this and said, “Don’t worry, Leah! Try again!”

Leah took a deep breath. She thought hard. She decided to make a strong base first. She put the blue crayon down flat, then the red crayon on top. She added the yellow crayon last. This time, the tower stood tall! Leah smiled and jumped with joy.

Everyone at school clapped for Leah. She learned that if you try again and think clever, you can build wonderful things. Leah was happy to share her crayons and play with her friends. They all built towers together, and they had a great day!

Tim was a small boy with a big smile. He loved to go to school every day. One sunny day, Tim went to the library. The library was a big room full of books. It was a cheerful place, and Tim liked to look at the pictures in the books. 

While Tim was there, he found a box. The box was full of colors. "What is this?" Tim asked. He opened the box and saw paint! Tim was so happy. He wanted to paint a picture. But Tim needed a friend to help him. 

Just then, Mia, a girl from his class, came in. Tim smiled at her. "Do you want to paint with me?" he asked. Mia's eyes lit up. "Yes!" she said. Together, they took the paint and made a big, bright picture of the sun and flowers. 

When they finished, the picture was cheerful and full of colors. The teacher walked in and clapped her hands. "What a nice picture!" she said. Tim and Mia felt proud. They were happy to have painted together and made something wonderful. The two friends laughed and danced around the library, filled with joy.

Anna went to school with her friend, Ben. They were very happy and played together. Today, their teacher gave them some homework. It was to count the stars in the sky! 

Anna and Ben were excited. They both loved stars. After school, they went to Anna’s house. They sat in the yard and looked up at the dark sky. The stars twinkled like little lights. "Let's count the stars together!" said Anna with a big smile.

They counted and counted. "One, two, three..." they said. They laughed and had fun. The stars were friendly, shining bright above them. They reached ten stars and danced with joy. 

When it was time to go home, Anna said, "Thank you for being my friend, Ben!" Ben smiled and said, "Thank you, Anna! I love counting stars with you." They both went home happy, their hearts full of friendship.

One sunny day, Sam went to school. Sam was happy to see his friends. They liked to play and laugh together. After a while, Sam saw a big, tall tree outside. Sam wanted to skip to the tree and touch its leaves.

When Sam got to the tree, he saw something on the ground. It was a drawing! It was a nice drawing of a sun and a big flower. Sam picked it up and showed it to his friends. They liked the drawing very much. Sam had an idea. He said, "Let’s all make our own drawings!"

So, Sam and his friends found paper and crayons. They drew many funny things: cats, dogs, and even rainbows. They all felt happy and worked together. At the end of the day, Sam learned that sharing and making things with friends is fun. 

When it was time to go home, Sam smiled. He had fun, made new drawings, and learned that sharing makes everyone happy. Sam waved goodbye to his friends and skipped all the way home.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lucy. Lucy loved to play at school with her friends. One sunny day, Lucy and her friend Sam sat under a big tree. The sun was warm, and they felt happy.

Lucy said, “Let’s make a song!” Sam smiled big and said, “Yes! I can listen!” They both took a deep breath and started to sing. Their song was about a butterfly flying in the sky. It was a silly song, and they laughed as they sang.

Other kids saw Lucy and Sam. They came over to listen too. Soon, everyone joined in. They sang together, and it was loud and fun. Lucy felt so happy! The warm sun shined on them as they laughed and played.

At the end of the day, Lucy looked at her friends and said, “I love our songs!” Everyone cheered and agreed. They were all friends, and they had a great time at school. Lucy knew that songs and friends made her heart feel warm.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Jake. Jake was happy and he loved to play with his friends. One day, Jake saw a big, silly puzzle on the table. He smiled and said, “Look at this puzzle! It is so silly!”

Jake's friend Mia came over. She had big, bright eyes and a big smile. “Hi, Jake! Can we do the puzzle together?” she asked. Jake nodded and said, “Yes! Let’s do it!” They both sat down and started to put the pieces together. 

As they worked, some pieces were hard to fit. They made some funny faces and laughed. “This piece is silly!” Jake said, holding a piece upside down. Mia giggled and said, “You are silly, Jake!” They both laughed so much that other kids came over to see what was happening.

After a while, with lots of giggles and fun, they finished the puzzle. It was a bright picture of a happy sun! “We did it!” Jake shouted. Mia smiled wide and said, “Yes! We are great friends!” They hugged, and the whole class cheered. Jake and Mia were so happy to share the silly puzzle and their smiles.

Mia was a little girl who loved to explore. One sunny day at school, her teacher said, "Let’s make a big painting!" Mia felt proud when she heard this. She wanted to help her friends, so she smiled and said, "I can help!"

Mia gathered all her friends. They all took big brushes and lots of colors. Mia said, "Let’s paint a big tree!" Everyone was excited and started to paint. They used green for the leaves and brown for the trunk. Mia helped her friends find the colors they needed. She said, "Here’s the blue!" and "Look, I have the red!"

While they painted, they laughed and sang. Mia loved working with her friends. They all felt proud of their big painting. When they finished, the teacher clapped her hands and said, "Wow! What a great painting you made together!"

At the end of the day, Mia and her friends stood by their painting with big smiles. They were happy and felt good for helping each other. Mia thought, "This is the best day!" And everyone agreed.

One sunny day at school, Ben was very excited. He saw his friends playing a game on the grass. The game looked fun, but Ben didn’t know how to play it yet. He wanted to join in, but he was a little shy.

Ben watched his friends. They ran, laughed, and had a great time together. Then, Ben had an idea! He could discover how to play the game. He walked over to his friends and asked, "Can you show me how to play?" His friends smiled and said yes! 

They showed Ben how to kick the ball and run fast. Ben tried it and felt happy. He laughed and ran with his friends. He discovered that playing together is fun and makes everyone feel good. 

At the end of the day, Ben was not shy anymore. He learned that asking for help is good! Now, Ben can play with his friends every day. They all felt excited to play together, and they had a lot of fun!

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Zoe. She was at school with her friends. Today, Zoe had a lesson about animals. She liked animals, but she felt a bit scared because the teacher said they would see a big snake in the lesson. 

When the lesson started, Zoe watched the snake in a glass box. It was long and shiny. Zoe thought it looked different, but then she saw how calm it was. The teacher told the kids that snakes can be nice and that they help the earth. Soon, Zoe felt less scared. 

After the lesson, Zoe told her friends what she learned. She said, "The snake is not scary! It is very cool!" Zoe felt proud. She liked to share her new knowledge. 

At the end of the day, Zoe went home and told her mom all about the snake. Zoe finished her day with a big smile. She learned that sometimes, things we first think are scary can be good. Zoe was happy to be brave.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Leo. Leo loved to play with his friends at school. One day, Leo saw a girl named Mia sitting all alone. She looked lonely. Leo wanted to help her feel better, so he walked over to her.

“Hi, Mia! Do you want to play with me?” Leo asked, smiling. Mia looked up and said, “Yes, I would like that.” Leo and Mia played a fun game together. They laughed and ran around. As they played, Leo remembered a story his teacher told him. The story was about being kind and helping others. Leo thought it was a good idea to share fun times with Mia.

After their game, Mia was happy and no longer felt lonely. She said, “Thank you, Leo! You are a good friend.” Leo felt warm inside because he helped Mia feel better. He learned that sharing smiles and fun is very good. 

From that day on, Leo and Mia played together every day. They made each other happy and helped others too. Leo remembered that being kind is a big and bright way to make friends. And so, they all had fun at school, happy and together.

Once there was a girl named Ella. Ella loved school, but one day she felt nervous. It was her turn to show her art to the class. She had made a big picture on paper. It had bright colors and a big sun, but she was scared.

When it was time, Ella walked to the front. Her hands shook a little, and her heart beat fast. But she took a deep breath and smiled. She began to show her picture to everyone. "Look!" she said, "This is my happy sun!"

The other kids clapped and smiled. They liked her picture! Ella felt happy and less nervous. One boy, Sam, said he wanted to draw too! Ella helped him by sharing her paper and crayons. Together, they made a big, fun picture of a day in the park.

At the end of the day, all the kids showed their art. They all felt happy and proud. Ella learned that sharing and helping others made her feel brave. School was fun, and everyone went home with a smile.

Max was a little boy. He was very happy. Max liked school. At school, he got to learn new things. He liked to learn about colors and shapes. Max had many friends. They played and laughed together. 

One day, Max saw a girl named Lily. She looked sad. Max wanted to help her. He went to her and said, “Hi, Lily! Do you want to play with us?” Lily smiled a little. She said, “Yes, I would like that.” Max was so happy to see her smile.

Max and Lily played with blocks. They built a tall tower. The tower fell down, but they did not mind. They laughed and tried again. Max learned that it was fun to help friends. When they played together, everyone was happy.

At the end of the day, Max and Lily were best friends. They learned that sharing and helping made them smile. Max was very happy. School was fun, especially with friends!

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. She went to a bright classroom. The sun shone through the big windows, making everything warm and happy. Lily loved to draw pictures with her crayons. 

One day, Lily saw her friend Tim. Tim looked sad. He had a paper but did not know how to draw a big red apple. Lily asked, “Do you want help?” Tim nodded. 

Lily sat with Tim and showed him how to draw the apple. They drew together and laughed. The apple looked so nice and bright! Tim smiled big and said, “Thank you, Lily!” 

At the end of the day, all the kids showed their pictures. Tim held up his apple with pride. Lily felt happy inside. Helping her friend made her day even brighter!

Tom was a kind boy. One sunny day at school, he went to the playground. He saw his friend, Mia. She looked sad. 

“Why are you sad, Mia?” Tom asked. 

“I can't read this book,” Mia said with a frown. 

Tom smiled. “I can help you! Let’s sit under the tree.” They sat down, and Tom showed Mia how to sound out the words. Mia’s face lit up. 

“Thank you, Tom! You are so kind!” she said. They read together and had lots of fun. After the book, they played on the swings and laughed. It was a happy day!

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Emma. She liked to go to school. Emma's teacher was kind and helped her learn. One day, the teacher said, “Let’s be quiet and write our names.” 

Emma sat at her desk with her sharp pencil. She was very quiet. She looked at the paper and took a deep breath. Then, she began to write her name. "E-M-M-A," she said softly. When she finished, she smiled big because she did it all by herself!

After everyone wrote their names, the teacher clapped her hands. “Good job, Emma!” she said. Emma felt happy. She learned that being quiet and trying hard helps us do great things. 

From that day on, Emma loved to write. She knew that if she stayed calm and worked hard, she could write anything. And that made school fun! Emma was proud, and she was always happy to learn with her teacher and friends.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Jack. Jack was a shy boy. He loved to draw but did not like to talk to others. One day, Jack took his red pencil and sat alone under a big tree.

While Jack was drawing a big sun, he saw a little cat. The cat was gray and soft. It was looking at him with big, curious eyes. Jack wanted to play, but he felt too shy. He watched the cat run around the tree, and he smiled.

Then something brave happened. Jack stood up and ran to the cat! "Hello, little cat!" he said. The cat meowed and ran to him. Jack felt happy in his heart. They played together, running and chasing each other.

Soon, Jack and the cat found other kids. They all laughed and played together. Jack was no longer shy. He drew pictures of the cat and the sun with his pencil. Jack felt good because he made new friends. His day at school was very happy!

Sofia was a curious girl. She loved to explore and play at school. One sunny day, her teacher said it was time for storytime. Sofia was excited! She listened closely as her teacher read from a big book.

After the story, Sofia wanted to play. She looked around the classroom. Then she saw a small box on a shelf. Sofia was very curious. "What is in that box?" she thought. She tiptoed over and opened the box. Inside, she found many colorful toys!

Sofia smiled big. She called her friends to come and see. "Look!" she said. "We can play with these toys!" Her friends were happy and they all played together. They built tall towers and had lots of fun. 

At the end of the day, Sofia felt very good. She discovered new toys and made her friends smile. She learned that being curious and playing can make everyone happy. Sofia loved her school!

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Will. Will was brave, and he loved to help his friends. One day, Will saw his friend, Sam, looking sad at his desk. Sam had a big problem. He lost his favorite toy, and he did not know where to find it.

Will wanted to help Sam feel better. He walked over and said, “Let’s look for your toy together!” Sam smiled a little, but he still felt sad. Will took Sam’s hand, and they looked around the school. They checked the playground, the art room, and even the lunchroom. 

Finally, near the big tree, they found a little red car stuck in the grass. It was Sam’s toy! Sam was so happy. “Thank you, Will! You are a good friend!” he said with a big smile. Will felt proud. He liked being brave and helping others. 

When they went back to the class, Will said, “Let’s share this toy!” They played together and made new games. All their friends joined in, and soon, the whole class was laughing and having fun. Will felt joy in his heart because helping Sam made them both happy. At the end of the day, they were all good friends.

Nora was a small girl with a big smile. One sunny day at school, it was time for lunch. Nora took her lunch box and sat down with her friends. “What do you have for lunch?” asked Tim, her friend.

“I have a sandwich and an apple,” said Nora. “What do you have?” Tim looked sad and said, “I have no food. I forgot my lunch at home.” 

Nora wanted to help her friend. “You can share my lunch!” she said. Tim’s face lit up with joy. “Thank you, Nora! You are so kind!” Nora smiled back and gave Tim half of her sandwich and her apple.

They both ate and talked, and soon everyone at the table laughed and had fun. “This is the best lunch!” said Tim. Nora felt happy inside because she helped her friend. They played together after lunch, and the day was bright and nice!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Dan. Dan had a big, blue backpack. It was full of fun things like crayons and books. Dan loved to play at school. Every day, he was happy to see his friends.

One sunny day, Dan saw his friend Lily looking sad. She lost her toy and could not find it. Dan wanted to help. He smiled and said, "Let's look together!" They walked in the gentle breeze, looking under swings and behind trees. But they still could not find the toy.

Dan had an idea. He took a deep breath and began to sing a happy song. “La la la!” he sang. Lily smiled and began to sing too. Soon, all their friends joined in! They sang together as they looked, and the song made everyone feel good.

Then, from behind a big rock, they heard a small sound. “Meow!” It was Lily’s toy cat! It was hiding! Lily was so happy. She hugged her toy tight. Dan felt proud. They all danced and sang in joy. At school that day, they helped a friend and ended with big smiles.

One sunny day at school, Ivy and her friend Ben sat at their big table. Ivy had a blue crayon, and Ben had a red crayon. "Look, Ben! Let’s build a house!" said Ivy with a big smile. Ben nodded and said, "Yes! A clever house!"

Ivy drew a big square for the house. Ben drew a nice door and two pretty windows. "Ivy, your blue crayon is so nice!" said Ben. "Thank you, Ben! Your red crayon is nice too!" said Ivy. They were happy to draw together. 

After they finished the house, Ivy said, "Let’s add a sun!" She took her yellow crayon and drew a smiling sun in the sky. “Wow! It looks great!” said Ben. "You are so clever, Ivy!" 

Ivy giggled and said, "No, we are clever together!" They both laughed and felt proud of their big, happy house. At the end of the day, Ivy and Ben were best friends, and they loved their art.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Kai. Kai loved to go to the library at his school. The library had many books and was a very cheerful place. The walls were bright and there were big, soft chairs to sit in. Kai liked to read about big animals and faraway lands.

One day, while Kai was reading, he saw a box. The box was very big and had many colors. "What is in the box?" Kai said with a smile. He opened the box and found paint! There were red, blue, and yellow paints all inside. Kai was very happy. He wanted to paint something nice.

Kai took the paints to a big white wall in the library. He thought and thought, then he started to paint. He painted happy suns, big trees, and smiling faces. When he was done, the wall looked bright and cheerful! All the kids at school saw the wall and clapped their hands. They loved it so much!

The teacher came in and smiled at Kai. "This is wonderful, Kai! You have made our library even more cheerful!" she said. Kai felt proud. He learned that sharing joy with others makes everyone happy. From that day on, Kai loved to paint and bring smiles to his friends. And they all played together in the cheerful library happily ever after.

Ruby loved school. She had a friendly smile and bright eyes. One day, Ruby sat at a big table with her friend Max. They were happy to be together. 

“Let’s count our homework!” said Ruby. She had a sheet of paper with numbers. Max nodded and said, “Yes! I like counting!” They looked at their papers and began to count. “One, two, three…” Ruby said. Max joined in, “Four, five, six!”

After they counted, Ruby said, “We did it! We are good at counting!” Max smiled and said, “Yes! We are great friends!” They both laughed. 

Then, their teacher came over. “Wow! You two are counting very well!” she said. Ruby and Max felt proud. They high-fived each other and said, “Yay!” They went home happy, excited to tell their families about their fun day at school.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Finn. Finn was very happy at school. He liked to play with his friends and make big drawings with colorful crayons. One sunny day, Finn saw a tall girl named Mia. She looked sad and could not reach her drawing on the top shelf.

Finn wanted to help Mia. He ran to her and said, "I can help you!" Then, Finn took a big breath and started to skip to the tall shelf. He was very brave! When he got there, he reached up high and got Mia's drawing. Mia's face lit up with joy. 

"Thank you, Finn!" Mia said with a big smile. Finn felt warm and happy inside. He loved to help his friends. They both sat down and shared their drawings. Finn drew a bright sun, and Mia drew a big tree.

At the end of the day, all the kids played together and felt good. Finn was glad he could help Mia, and they all laughed and skipped around the playground. It was a great day at school!

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Luna. She went to school every day. Luna loved to play with her friends and learn new things. One sunny day, the sun felt warm on her face, and she was very happy.

In class, Luna sat next to her best friend, Mia. The teacher said, “Let’s listen to a song!” Luna and Mia smiled big. They loved music. The song was fun and made them dance in their seats. They sang along with joy.

After the song, Luna and Mia held hands. “That was great!” Luna said. Mia nodded and said, “Let’s sing that song again at recess!” They both laughed and ran outside together. 

At recess, Luna and Mia sang the song loudly. Other kids joined in, and they all played together. The school felt warm with laughter and friendship. At the end of the day, Luna was happy. She had fun with her friend, and they loved to listen to their song.

Owen was a little boy who loved to play at school. One day, his teacher brought out a big puzzle. "Let's work on this together!" she said with a smile. Owen was very excited. He loved puzzles! 

As they started to put the puzzle pieces together, Owen noticed that one piece was missing. "Oh no! Where is it?" he asked, looking all around. His friend Mia said, "Maybe it is being silly and hiding!" They both started to laugh. The other kids joined in, and soon the whole class was giggling.

Then, they looked under the table and found the lost piece! Owen rushed back to the puzzle with a big smile. "Hooray!" he shouted. With the last piece in place, the puzzle was complete. It was a big picture of a sunny day with flowers and happy animals. 

Owen learned that sometimes things can be silly, but it is fun to laugh with friends. They all felt happy when they finished the puzzle together. "Teamwork makes us strong!" said their teacher. And on that day, Owen and his friends felt very proud and joyful.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Maya. She loved to paint. One sunny day at school, her teacher said, “Today, we will explore colors!” Maya smiled and said, “Yay! I love colors!” She was very proud to join her friends.

Maya and her friends took big brushes and bright paints. They painted big sunshine, tall trees, and happy flowers. Maya said, “Look at my painting! It is a big, red apple!” Her friend Sam pointed and said, “Wow, Maya! That is so nice!” Maya felt even prouder.

After they painted, the teacher said, “Let’s hang our art on the wall!” Maya helped her friends put up their paintings. When they were done, the wall was so bright and happy! Maya said, “We did it together!” 

The teacher smiled and said, “You all did a great job! Remember, working together makes art even better!” Maya was happy to hear this. She learned that sharing and helping friends is the best way to explore and create.

One sunny day at school, Cole was playing with his friends. They were loud and happy. Cole saw a big box in the corner. “What is in the box?” he asked. His friends looked at him with big eyes. 

“Let’s open it!” said Mia, a girl with curly hair. Cole and Mia ran to the box. They pulled the lid off and looked inside. “Wow!” shouted Cole. “Look at all the toys!” Inside were balls, blocks, and a funny game. Cole was so excited!

“This is the best day ever!” said Cole. He took out the funny game. “Let’s play it!” His friends cheered. They all sat in a big circle. They laughed and played the game together. 

After a fun time, Cole smiled and said, “I love school!” All his friends agreed. They felt happy and excited because they discovered a new game together. It was a great day at school!

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Chloe. She loved her school and her friends. One day, Chloe had a lesson about sharing. Some kids were scared to share their toys. They thought it would be hard.

Chloe saw her friend Sam looking scared. She went to him and said, "It is okay, Sam! We can share together!" Sam smiled and felt better. Chloe took a toy and gave it to Sam. He took a toy and gave it to her. They were happy!

When the lesson was over, all the kids played together. They laughed and shared their toys. Chloe and Sam had fun. They were no longer scared. 

At the end of the day, they all said, "We did it! We finished the lesson!" Chloe felt proud of her friends. They learned that sharing is fun, and now, they were best friends.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Jude. Jude went to school every day, but he felt very lonely. He did not have a friend to play with. One day, Jude sat by himself on a big swing. He looked sad.  

A teacher saw Jude and asked, “What is wrong, Jude?” Jude said, “I feel lonely. I want a friend.” The teacher smiled and said, “Let’s tell a story to help you remember something special.” Jude's eyes brightened. He loved stories!  

The teacher told a tale about friendship. She said, “When you share and play, you can make a friend.” Jude listened closely. He thought, “I want to share and play!” When the bell rang, he ran to the playground.   

Jude saw a girl sitting alone. He walked over and said, “Hi! Do you want to play with me?” The girl smiled and said, “Yes!” They had fun together, swinging and laughing. Jude felt so happy. He remembered the story and found a friend.  Now, he was never lonely again!

One day, Leah sat in class. She looked at her paper. It was blank and white. Leah felt nervous. She wanted to show her picture, but she didn’t know what to draw. 

Leah saw her friend Sam. He was looking sad because he had no paper. Leah wanted to help him. She smiled and said, “You can use my paper!” Sam's eyes got big. He was so happy! 

Together, they drew bright sun and big trees. Leah showed Sam how to use pretty colors. They laughed and had fun. Soon, their papers were full of happy pictures. 

When the teacher came, she smiled. She said, “Wow! Great job, Leah and Sam!” Leah felt happy now. She was not nervous anymore. It was a good day at school!

Tim was a happy boy. One day, he went to school. “I like school!” said Tim with a big smile. He saw his friend, Sam. “Hi, Sam! Do you want to learn with me?” Tim asked.

“Yes! I want to learn,” Sam said. They went to the big room where their teacher was. “Today, we will learn about animals,” said the teacher. “Yay!” said Tim. He loved animals. 

Tim and Sam looked at pictures of cats and dogs. “Look! This cat is soft,” said Tim. “And this dog can run fast!” said Sam. They learned many fun things about animals. 

When school was done, Tim said, “This was a happy day! I like to learn with my friends!” Sam smiled and said, “Me too!” They waved goodbye as they left school, excited to come back the next day.

Anna went to school on a bright day. She loved her classroom. It was full of colors and happy pictures. “I want to draw,” Anna said to her friend Max. “Let’s draw together!”

Anna and Max got their big paper and crayons. “What will you draw?” asked Max. “I will draw a big sun,” said Anna with a smile. Max thought for a moment. “I will draw a tall tree!” he said. They both started to draw and filled the paper with colors.

When they finished, Anna showed her sun to all the kids. “Look! It is bright and happy!” she said. Max showed his tree. “Look at my tree! It is very tall!” The other kids clapped and cheered. “Good job, Anna and Max!”

Anna felt very happy. She learned that sharing fun things makes smiles. “Let’s draw more tomorrow!” said Anna. “Yes!” said Max. And they both giggled as they cleaned up. 

The moral of the story is: Sharing makes happy moments even better!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Sam. Sam was very kind. He went to school every day. At school, there was a big playground. The playground had swings, slides, and lots of friends to play with.

One sunny day, Sam found a book on the ground next to the playground. It was a bright book with pictures of animals. Sam picked it up and said, "I can read this!" He opened the book and looked at all the fun pictures. There were cats, dogs, and birds in the book. Sam smiled wide.

Sam called his friends over to see the book. "Look what I found!" he said. They all gathered around and listened as Sam read the book out loud. The friends laughed at the silly animals. They felt happy as they sat together.

After reading, Sam and his friends ran to the swings and slides. They played and played until the sun began to set. Sam felt good because he shared the book, and everyone had fun. It was a special day at school, and they all went home with big smiles.

Once upon a time, in a small school, there was a girl named Lucy. Lucy loved to play with her friend, Sam. They liked to laugh and have fun together. But sometimes, they had to be quiet, like when they were in class with their teacher.

One day, the teacher said, "Today we will write!" Lucy felt happy. She loved to write. Sam sat next to her. They smiled at each other. The teacher gave them paper and crayons. Lucy wrote her name and drew a big sun. Sam wrote his name and drew a blue cat.

While they wrote, they helped each other. Lucy showed Sam how to draw grass. Sam helped Lucy find a nice crayon. They felt good working together. Soon, the teacher said, "Time is up!" Lucy and Sam looked at their pictures and clapped their hands. 

The teacher smiled and said, "Great job, Lucy and Sam!" They felt proud. After class, they played outside. They ran and laughed, happy to be friends. The sun shone bright, and the day was full of joy.

Once there was a boy named Jake. He was a good boy, but he was very shy. Jake liked to sit at his desk and draw with his pencil. He liked to draw big trees and small birds. But Jake did not like to talk to other kids.

One day, at school, Jake saw a girl named Lily. She looked sad. Jake wanted to help her, but he was too shy to say anything. Then, he saw that Lily did not have a pencil. She needed a pencil to draw. Jake thought for a moment and then he decided to help her. He took a deep breath and said, “Do you want to use my pencil?” 

Lily smiled big and said, “Yes, please!” Jake felt happy. He gave her the pencil, and she started to draw. Jake watched her draw a nice rainbow. Soon, other kids came over to see. They all liked Lily’s drawing. Jake felt brave and joined in. 

Now, Jake was happy and had new friends. He learned that helping others made him feel good. Being shy was okay, but being kind was even better. After that day, Jake would always share his pencil. He liked to run around and play with his friends. Jake saw that friends are a treasure.

Mia was a curious girl. One sunny day, she went to school with her big backpack. Inside her backpack, she had a new book. It was a book about stars and moons. Mia was very excited to read it.

At school, Mia sat on the soft rug. She opened her book and stared at the bright pages. Just then, she saw a boy named Tom. Tom was lonely and did not play with anyone. Mia wanted to help him. She smiled and said, “Do you want to see my book?”

Tom’s face lit up. “Yes, I want to see!” he said. Mia showed Tom the pictures of stars and moons. They laughed and pointed at the shiny lights. They both felt happy and forgot about being lonely.

After the story, Mia and Tom decided to play together. They danced around the room like stars in the sky. They played until the bell rang. Mia and Tom became good friends. They knew they would have many fun days at school, full of books and play.

Once upon a time, in a big school, there was a boy named Ben. Ben had a brave heart. He liked to play with his friends and help them when they needed it. One day, Ben saw that his friend Mia was sad. She had a big problem with her art project at her desk. 

Ben wanted to help Mia. He walked over to her and said, “Let’s share my crayons!” Mia smiled and felt happier. Together, they made pretty pictures with bright colors. Ben felt proud that he could help his friend. They both laughed and had fun at the desk.

While they were working, they found a shiny sticker on the floor. “Look!” said Ben. “We can share this sticker too!” Mia’s eyes sparkled with joy. They decided to put the sticker on their art project, and it made it even better! 

At the end of the day, their teacher came to see their art. She said, “Wow! You two are brave and kind to share.” Ben and Mia beamed with happiness. They learned that sharing made their day special. They left school with big smiles, ready for more fun tomorrow.

Zoe was a small girl who went to school. One sunny day, Zoe brought her lunch to school. She had a big sandwich, an apple, and some juice. But oh no! Zoe saw her friend, Max, looking sad. He did not have any lunch. 

Zoe wanted to help Max. She thought for a moment and then said, "You can have some of my lunch!" Max smiled wide and said, "Thank you, Zoe!" Zoe gave him half of her sandwich and her apple. They sat together and shared the food. 

When they finished, Max felt happy. He said, "You are a good friend, Zoe!" Zoe felt warm inside. It was nice to help. 

At the end of the day, Zoe and Max played together. They laughed and ran around. Zoe learned that sharing with friends is good. Helping others is what makes our hearts feel big and happy!

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Leo. Leo had a big, blue backpack. He loved his backpack because it had a picture of a sun on it. One day, Leo went to school and saw his friend Mia. “Hi, Mia!” Leo said with a smile. “Look at my backpack!”

Mia smiled back. “Wow, Leo! I like it! Can we sing a song?” she asked. Leo clapped his hands. “Yes! Let’s sing the gentle song!” They stood together in a sunny corner of the playground. Leo and Mia started to sing. “Twinkle, twinkle, little star, how I wonder what you are!” Their voices were sweet, and the sun felt warm.

Other kids heard them singing and ran over. “Can we sing too?” asked Sam. “Yes!” shouted Leo. The kids all joined in, and they sang together. The gentle song made everyone happy. Their laughter filled the air, and Leo felt joyful in his big, blue backpack.

When the song was over, the sun shone even brighter. “That was fun!” said Mia. “Let’s do it again tomorrow!” Leo nodded. “Yes, let’s sing every day!” And with big smiles, all the kids ran back to class, ready for more fun. Leo was very happy, and he knew school was a great place.

Once upon a time, in a bright class at school, there was a girl named Ella. Ella was very clever. One day, her teacher said, “Let’s build a tower with blocks!” Ella was excited. She loved to build.

Ella took her blocks and started to stack them high. “Look, I can build a tall tower!” she said with a big smile. But then, her tower fell down! “Oh no!” Ella cried. She felt sad.

Then her friend Max said, “Don’t cry, Ella! We can build it again. Let’s do it together!” Ella smiled again. She took her crayon and drew a plan on paper. “We can make it strong!” she said. Max nodded and they worked hard.

After a little while, they built a big, strong tower. Ella and Max jumped up and down. “We did it!” Ella shouted. Their teacher clapped and said, “You both are very clever!” Ella and Max felt happy. 

The lesson for the day was clear: When we work together, we can build anything! And with that, everyone in class smiled and played.

One sunny day, Max went to school. Max was very happy. He liked to go to the library. The library had many books and big, soft chairs. Max smiled and said, “Look at all the books!”

Max’s teacher, Miss Lily, smiled back. “Yes, Max! And today we will paint a big picture!” Max’s eyes got big. “What will we paint?” he asked. Miss Lily said, “We will paint a cheerful sun and blue skies!”

Max was excited. He took his paintbrush and dipped it in yellow paint. “I love yellow!” he said. He painted a big, round sun. The sun looked so bright and happy. Miss Lily went around and said, “Good job, Max! Your sun is very cheerful!”

When they finished, all the kids showed their work. Max looked at his friends’ paintings too. “Wow! You all painted great!” he said. Miss Lily clapped her hands. “Let’s hang them up in the library!” Everyone cheered. Max felt warm and happy inside. “This is the best day!” he said with a big smile.

Lily was in her classroom at school. She liked school very much. Her teacher, Miss Joy, had a friendly smile. “Today, we will count!” Miss Joy said. Lily clapped her hands and said, “Yay! I love to count!”

After counting, Miss Joy gave everyone their homework. “This is your homework,” she said with a smile. “You will count the stars you see at night.” Lily was excited. “I can count stars!” she said. Her friend Max sat next to her. “I will count too!” he said. 

Later, when school was done, Lily and Max walked home together. “What will you count tonight?” Max asked. “I will count all the stars!” Lily said. “I will count the big ones and the small ones!” 

When Lily got home, she looked out at the sky. The stars were bright and shiny. She counted, “One, two, three…” and smiled. Counting stars was fun! Lily felt happy because she had a friendly day at school.

Tom was at school. He sat at his desk and looked at a big piece of paper. “I want to make a drawing!” he said with a big smile. 

Tom picked up his crayons and started to draw. He drew a sun, a tree, and a tall house. “Look at my drawing!” Tom called to his friend, Sam. 

“Wow, Tom! That is a tall house!” Sam said. “Can you skip with me after this?” Tom nodded and said, “Yes! Let’s skip!”

When they finished their work, Tom and Sam went outside. They held hands and began to skip on the grass. “This is fun!” shouted Tom. They both laughed and skipped all the way to the swings. They had a great day at school!

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a girl named Emma. Emma had a big smile and liked to play. She loved to share toys with her friends. One sunny day, Emma saw her friend Mia sitting alone. Emma wanted to help Mia feel better.

“Come play with me!” Emma said. Mia looked up and smiled. They played with blocks and built a tall tower. It was so much fun! Emma then said, “Let’s listen to a song!” Mia nodded, happy to join. They sat on the soft grass and listened to the warm song that came from the school.

As the song played, Emma and Mia sang together. Their voices were sweet and happy. They laughed and danced, feeling the warm sun on their faces. Other kids saw them and wanted to join, too! Soon, many friends were there, singing and dancing.

At the end of the song, everyone cheered! Emma and Mia felt full of joy. They hugged each other and said, “I am glad we are friends!” The day ended with happy hearts. Emma knew that friends make every day brighter.

One sunny day, Jack went to school. He was happy to see his friends. They played games and had fun together. But then, Jack saw a big puzzle on the table. It was a silly puzzle with lots of colors. 

Jack wanted to help his friend Emma. Emma looked sad because she could not find the right pieces for the puzzle. Jack smiled and said, “Let’s work together!” Emma’s face brightened up. They found the pieces and started to fit them together.

As they worked, they laughed and talked. They found a piece that looked like a funny cat. Jack said, “Look! Silly cat!” Emma laughed, too. Soon, they put all the pieces in place. The puzzle was done! 

All their friends came to see. They clapped and cheered for Jack and Emma. They were so happy! Jack felt proud. Helping a friend made him smile. It was a great day at school!

Sofia went to school one sunny day. She was very happy. Today was a special day because the class was going to explore! Sofia loved to explore. She held her friend Leo's hand. Together, they were ready for fun.

The teacher said they would find all the colors in the room. Sofia looked at the big, bright painting on the wall. It had red, blue, and yellow. Sofia smiled because she was proud of the nice painting. She told Leo, “Look! That painting has so many colors!”

After looking, Sofia and Leo found purple crayons. They wanted to make their own painting. They drew stars, flowers, and a big sun. Sofia loved it! When the teacher saw their work, she said, “Wow! What a great painting!” Sofia and Leo felt proud together.

At the end of the day, Sofia and Leo shared their painting with their friends. Everyone clapped and smiled. Sofia felt happy in her heart. She knew that exploring and making art with a friend was the best fun of all.

Once upon a time, in a bright and happy school, there was a boy named Will. Will loved to play games with his friends. One sunny day, Will found a big box in the playground. He was very excited! He opened the box to see what was inside. 

Inside the box, Will discovered a fun game with big, colorful pieces. He wanted to play the game with his friends. But when he looked around, he saw that some kids were sad. They did not have anyone to play with. Will stopped and thought. He wanted to share the fun!

Will said, “Let’s play this game together!” The sad kids smiled and came over. Will helped them learn the game. They all laughed and played together. The game was more fun with everyone. 

At the end of the day, all the kids were happy. Will felt good in his heart. He learned that helping others is a great way to have fun too. They all played together, and they all smiled. The end.

Nora went to school one bright day. She had a lesson about shapes. At first, she felt scared. What if she could not learn? What if the shapes were too hard? But Nora took a deep breath and said, "I can do this!"

The teacher showed a big circle, a small square, and a tall triangle. Nora looked at the shapes and started to draw. The more she drew, the less scared she felt. She saw that shapes were fun! When Nora finished her drawing, she was so proud. She smiled big and showed her friends.

“Look at my shapes!” she said. Her friends cheered for her. Nora discovered that trying new things could be fun and not scary at all. She learned that she could do hard things if she just tried. 

At the end of the day, Nora felt happy. She had fun with her friends and had a great lesson. Nora decided that being brave was the best way to learn. She could not wait for her next lesson!

Once upon a time at school, there was a boy named Dan. Dan was a nice boy, but today he felt lonely. He looked around and saw his friends playing. They were laughing and having fun, but Dan did not know how to join them.

Dan sat on a bench and thought about a story his mom told him. “If you want to make friends, you must be brave,” she said. Dan tried to remember her words. He took a deep breath and stood up. He said to his friends, “Can I play too?”

The kids smiled and said, “Yes, Dan! Come join us!” Dan felt happy. They played games and ran around together. Dan was not lonely anymore, and he had so much fun. 

At the end of the day, Dan smiled and said, “I remember my mom's story! Being brave helped me.” His friends cheered, and they all went home together, happy and full of joy.

Ivy was a little girl who went to school. One day, she felt very nervous. She had to show her class a big, white paper. The paper was blank, and Ivy did not know what to do with it.

Ivy sat at her desk and looked at her paper. She thought and thought. Then, her friend Max smiled at her. He had colorful crayons! Ivy felt a tiny bit better. “Can I use your crayons?” she asked Max. He gave her some crayons to share.

With the crayons, Ivy drew a big, bright sun and a green tree. She was so happy! The paper was not blank anymore. When it was her turn to show the class, Ivy held up her bright paper. Everyone clapped and smiled. 

Ivy was no longer nervous. She felt proud of her drawing. She learned that with friends and colors, she could make something nice. School was fun, and she loved to show her work!

Once there was a boy named Kai. He was a happy boy who loved to go to school. Every day, he learned new things like numbers, letters, and colors. Kai liked to play with his friends and share fun games. 

One sunny day at school, Kai saw a little girl named Mia. She looked sad. Kai went to her and asked, "What’s wrong, Mia?" Mia said, "I don’t know how to count." Kai thought for a moment, then he had a great idea! 

Kai said, "Let’s learn to count together!" They sat under a big tree and started to count. Kai used his fingers and counted out loud. One, two, three! Mia smiled and tried to count too. Soon, she was happy because she learned how to count! 

At the end of the day, they played a counting game with their friends. Everyone was happy. Kai felt good because he helped Mia learn something new. School was even more fun when they played together, and they all left with big smiles!

In a bright classroom, there was a girl named Ruby. Ruby loved to draw. One day, her teacher said, "Okay, kids! Let’s draw our favorite animals!" Ruby smiled big and got her crayons.

Ruby started to draw a big cat. But then she saw her friend, Sam, looking sad. "What is wrong, Sam?" Ruby asked. Sam said, "I can't draw a dog." Ruby thought for a moment and then said, "I can help you!"

Ruby took her crayons and sat next to Sam. "Look! First, draw the big head," she said. Sam tried to draw, but it was not quite right. "Like this!" Ruby showed him how. Sam smiled and said, "Wow! Thank you, Ruby!"

With Ruby’s help, Sam drew a nice dog. The classroom was bright, and the kids were happy. After they finished, their teacher said, "Great job, kids!" Ruby and Sam smiled at each other, feeling proud. They helped each other, and now they could both draw!

Finn was a kind boy. He loved to go to school. At school, there was a big playground where all the kids liked to play. Finn liked to slide and swing. One sunny day, he saw something shiny in the grass.

Finn walked over to the shiny thing. It was a small book! Finn picked up the book and opened it. The pages were full of fun pictures and words. He loved to read. Finn sat on the grass and read the book with a big smile.

As he read, his friends saw him. They came over to see what Finn had found. "What is it?" they asked. Finn showed them the book and said, "Look! Let’s read together!" The kids sat in a circle and took turns reading the funny stories. They laughed and had so much fun!

When the bell rang, Finn and his friends were happy. They not only had fun on the playground but also found a special book to share. Finn felt proud to be kind and to share with his friends. They all went home with big smiles on their faces.

Luna sat in her class. She looked at her teacher, Ms. Lee. Ms. Lee said, “Let’s write today! Who likes to write?” 

“I do!” said Luna. She liked to write. She liked to use big letters and little letters. Ms. Lee smiled and said, “Good, Luna! Let’s be very quiet while we write.” 

Luna took out her paper and pencil. She wrote a big sun and a blue sky. She wrote, “I love school!” Luna felt happy. She looked at her friends. They were all very quiet too. They were all writing nice things! 

When they were done, Ms. Lee said, “Now, let’s show our pictures!” Luna showed her sun. Her friends clapped and cheered. Luna smiled wide. 

The lesson was fun and kind. Luna learned that writing can bring joy. It is good to share with friends.

Owen was a shy boy. He liked to play with his toys but did not like to talk to other kids. One day, at school, Owen had a new pencil. It was bright red and very sharp. He loved it!

At recess, the other kids ran and laughed. Owen watched from the swings. His heart wanted to join, but he felt too shy. Then, he saw a girl drop her blue ball. She looked sad. Owen wanted to help.

He took a deep breath and ran over to her. “Here is your ball!” he said with a small smile. The girl smiled back and said thank you. Soon, more kids came over. They wanted to play with Owen and the girl.

Owen felt happy. He was no longer shy. He ran and played with his new friends. From that day on, Owen learned that sometimes, it’s good to be brave and say hello. It made him feel warm inside!

Maya was a curious girl. She loved to learn new things. One day at school, her teacher brought a big book. The book had bright pictures and fun stories. Maya wanted to play with her friends, but she decided to look at the book first.

Maya opened the book and saw a picture of a big, red apple. She thought, "Wow! I want to know more!" Her friends came over. They were curious too! Together, they looked at the book and found more pictures. There were flowers, animals, and even a rainbow!

As they looked at the book, they started to play. They pretended to be animals in the pictures. Maya was a happy bird, and her friends were bunnies and frogs. They laughed and played together, and they learned so much about the world.

At the end of the day, Maya felt very happy. She learned that books are fun and can make playtime better. She hugged the book and smiled at her friends. "Let’s look at more books tomorrow!" she said with joy. And they all agreed, excited for their next adventure.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Cole. Cole was a brave boy with a big heart. He loved to play with his friends and learn new things. One day, he saw a new boy at school. His name was Sam, and he looked a bit shy.

Cole wanted to be friends with Sam. He walked over to him and said, "Hi, I am Cole! Do you want to play?" Sam smiled but did not say much. Cole thought Sam might be sad because he had no one to play with. So, Cole had a great idea. He said, "Let’s share the desk! We can draw and have fun together!"

Sam’s eyes lit up. He was happy to share the desk with Cole. They sat close and drew pictures of big blue skies and happy suns. As they drew, they talked and laughed. Cole felt good because he was brave and made a new friend. Sam felt good too because he was no longer shy.

From that day on, Cole and Sam played together every day at school. They shared toys, lunch, and lots of smiles. They were best friends, and they were very happy. Being brave and sharing made their hearts feel warm!

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Chloe. Chloe was in a big school with many friends. One sunny day, it was time for lunch. Chloe took a small box from her bag. Inside was a yummy sandwich and an apple.

“Wow! You have a small lunch!” said her friend Sam. “Can I have some?” Chloe looked at her food and smiled. “Sure, Sam! I can help you!” She gave him half of her sandwich and her apple.

Sam was happy. “Thank you, Chloe! You are nice!” Chloe felt warm in her heart. She knew that sharing made everyone feel good. 

After lunch, their teacher said, “Chloe helped her friend today! Helping is kind.” Chloe smiled big, and all her friends clapped. They all had fun and felt happy together. Chloe learned that sharing and helping makes school a better place.

At school, Jude had a bright red backpack. It was not big, but it was very nice. Jude loved to help his friends. One day, he saw Mia sitting by herself. She looked sad. "What is wrong, Mia?" Jude asked gently.

Mia said, "I lost my teddy bear. I want to play, but I feel sad." Jude thought for a moment. "Let’s look for your teddy bear together!" he said with a smile. Mia smiled back. They went all around the playground. They looked under swings and by the big slide.

After a little while, Jude heard a soft sound. "What is that?" Jude asked. He listened closely, and then he said, "I hear a song!" They walked toward the sound, and there was Mia’s teddy bear! It was sitting next to a big tree, singing a gentle song.

Mia jumped with joy! "You found my teddy!" she said. Jude said, "Let’s sing together!" So they both sang a happy song with the teddy bear. Jude and Mia had fun playing and laughing, and they knew they were great friends. The day ended with smiles and hugs, and everyone felt very happy!

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Leah. Leah was clever and loved to help her friends at school. One day, her teacher asked the class to build a big, happy house with blocks. Leah was excited!

Leah got her blocks and started to build. But her friend, Max, was sad. He had no blocks to build with. Leah saw Max and wanted to help him. She ran to him and said, “Come, Max! You can use my blocks.”

Max smiled and took some blocks. They both worked together. Leah used her red crayon to draw pretty flowers on the house. Max used his blue crayon to draw a sunny sky. They built the best house ever!

When they finished, their teacher clapped and everyone cheered. Leah and Max were so happy. They learned that helping each other made their fun time at school even better.

Tim was a happy boy. One day, he went to school. Tim said, “I love school!” His friend Mia smiled and said, “Me too! What do we do today?” 

Their teacher, Miss Lee, said, “Today, we will paint in the library!” Tim’s eyes got big. “Paint? Yay!” he cheered. Mia jumped up and down. “I want to paint a big sun!”

In the library, Miss Lee gave each child a big paper and colorful paint. She said, “Let’s make cheerful pictures!” Tim painted a bright yellow sun with a big smile. Mia painted flowers that were pink and blue. They both laughed and had fun.

When they were done, Miss Lee looked at their work. “Wow! You both did a great job!” she said. Tim and Mia smiled wide. “Thank you, Miss Lee!” they said. They felt proud and happy. 

At the end of the day, Tim told Mia, “Today was the best day!” Mia nodded. “Yes! I love to paint!” They left school with big smiles, ready for more fun tomorrow.

Anna was a happy girl. She liked school because her teacher was friendly. Every day, Anna learned new things. One day, the teacher gave the class some homework. The homework was to count how many books they had at home.

After school, Anna went home. She looked at her books. She had many books! Anna started to count. "One, two, three," she said, and she smiled. Counting was fun! But she wanted to help her friend, Max. Max was sad because he did not have many books.
